title: Llama7B dense compute ceiling envelope V2
hypothesis: The measured dense FP16 GEMM 8x8 tile density of about 270 MAC/cycle/mm2 changes the Llama7B attention/KV frontier enough that the 131072 MAC/cycle HBM-bound floor may become reachable for large die and high logic-area budgets.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the registered dense GEMM tile density make the Llama7B 131072 MAC/cycle HBM-bound floor physically plausible under the current die-size and logic-fraction envelope?
include: Registered dense FP16 GEMM tile measurements from PR #760
Updated design-registry comparison claim for dense tile versus nm64_flat
Measured best density selected from internal registry records
Existing 150 and 300 MAC/cycle/mm2 analytic envelopes for continuity
exclude: SRAM bank placement or HBM PHY/controller timing
follow_on_broad_sweep: If measured dense density reaches or nearly reaches the floor, plan dense tile scaling and producer/memory coupling
If only the 300 envelope reaches the floor, measure larger/pipelined dense tile variants before detailed NoC work
If measured dense density remains far short, revise die/context assumptions or precision strategy

expected_benefit: Converts dense-tile PPA evidence into a Llama7B frontier decision
Keeps measured internal density distinct from analytic 150/300 envelopes
Provides a measured basis for deciding whether to scale dense compute macros
risks: Dense 8x8 is still a macro-style PPA harness and not a full NPU datapath
The density may not hold at larger tile sizes or when connected to SRAM/NoC
The result is an analytic planning gate, not a routed full-chip implementation

required_evaluations.objective: Rerun the Llama7B attention/KV compute ceiling envelope using the registered dense FP16 GEMM tile density as the measured RTLGen best.

expected_result.reason: The job should show which die and logic budgets become feasible when the dense 8x8 tile replaces nm64_flat as measured best compute density.
